description_long: This is a small, single-story wooden cabin with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The exterior walls are constructed from light-colored horizontal wooden planks, with visible interlocking log corners. The front facade features a set of four square windows grouped together and a single open doorway leading onto a small, uncovered porch area. The side of the cabin has a large rectangular window, while the rear and opposite side are windowless, emphasizing privacy. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground on concrete footings, giving it a sturdy and rustic appearance suitable for residential or recreational use.
description: A compact wooden cabin with a gable shingle roof, grouped front windows, and a small open porch, elevated on concrete footings.
description_view_0: Front view showing four grouped windows, an open doorway, and a small porch area.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a large rectangular window and the porch extending from the front.
description_view_2: Rear view with no windows or doors, showing plain wooden planks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, also windowless, highlighting the cabin's simple wooden construction.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in reddish-brown asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: One set of four small square windows grouped together on the front facade, and one large rectangular window on the left side; windows are symmetrically arranged on their respective sides
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located on the right side of the front facade, adjacent to the porch area
